{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_103d30bd112c41619898c969","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope-Based Key Tracking Demonstration\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This example demonstrates the hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n4\t//! system for YAML parsing, showing how it properly handles nested\n5\t//! mappings and avoids false duplicate positives.\n6\t\n7\tuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\n8\tuse std::fmt;\n9\t\n10\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n11\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n12\tstruct Scope {\n13\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n14\t    indent_level: usize,\n15\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n16\t    keys: HashSet<String>,\n17\t    /// Line number where this scope started\n18\t    start_line: usize,\n19\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n20\t    parent_key: Option<String>,\n21\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n22\t    is_flow_style: bool,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\timpl Scope {\n26\t    /// Create a new scope\n27\t    fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n28\t        Self {\n29\t            indent_level,\n30\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n31\t            start_line,\n32\t            parent_key,\n33\t            is_flow_style: false,\n34\t        }\n35\t    }\n36\t\n37\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n38\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n39\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n40\t    }\n41\t\n42\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n43\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n44\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n45\t    }\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n49\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n50\tstruct ScopeStack {\n51\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n52\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n53\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n54\t    base_indent: usize,\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\timpl ScopeStack {\n58\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n59\t    fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n60\t        Self {\n61\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n62\t            base_indent,\n63\t        }\n64\t    }\n65\t\n66\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n67\t    fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n68\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n69\t    }\n70\t\n71\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n72\t    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n73\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n77\t    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n78\t        // Calculate scope level from indentation\n79\t        let scope_level = indent_level / self.base_indent;\n80\t\n81\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n82\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n83\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n84\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n85\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n86\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n87\t\n88\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n89\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n90\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n91\t        } else {\n92\t            // Create new scope\n93\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n94\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n95\t        }\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n99\t    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n100\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n101\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n102\t\n103\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n104\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n105\t            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n106\t            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n107\t            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n108\t        }\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n112\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n113\t        self.scopes.last()\n114\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n115\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n119\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n120\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n121\t\n122\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n123\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n124\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n125\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n126\t                key: key.to_string(),\n127\t                scope_path,\n128\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n129\t                duplicate_line: line,\n130\t            })\n131\t        } else {\n132\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n133\t            scope.add_key(key);\n134\t            Ok(())\n135\t        }\n136\t    }\n137\t\n138\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n139\t    fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n140\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n141\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n142\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n143\t                path.push(key.clone());\n144\t            }\n145\t        }\n146\t        path.join(\".\")\n147\t    }\n148\t\n149\t    /// Get current indent level\n150\t    fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n151\t        self.scopes.last()\n152\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n153\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n154\t    }\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n158\t#[derive(Debug)]\n159\tstruct DuplicateKeyError {\n160\t    key: String,\n161\t    scope_path: String,\n162\t    first_line: usize,\n163\t    duplicate_line: usize,\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\timpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n167\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n168\t        write!(\n169\t            f,\n170\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n171\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n172\t        )\n173\t    }\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t/// Key context classification\n177\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n178\tenum KeyContext {\n179\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n180\t    InlineScalar { key: String, value: String },\n181\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n182\t    ParentMapping { key: String },\n183\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n184\t    ParentSequence { key: String },\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t/// Extract key context from a line\n188\tfn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n189\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n190\t\n191\t    // Find colon position\n192\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n193\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n194\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n195\t\n196\t    // Skip if key is empty\n197\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n198\t    if key.is_empty() {\n199\t        return None;\n200\t    }\n201\t\n202\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n203\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n204\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n205\t    } else {\n206\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n207\t            key: key.to_string(),\n208\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n209\t        }\n210\t    };\n211\t\n212\t    Some(context)\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\t/// Get leading whitespace length\n216\tfn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n217\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n218\t}\n219\t\n220\t/// Process YAML lines and detect duplicate keys\n221\tfn process_yaml(yaml: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, DuplicateKeyError> {\n222\t    let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n223\t    let mut processed_keys = Vec::new();\n224\t\n225\t    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n226\t        let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n227\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n228\t\n229\t        // Skip empty lines and comments\n230\t        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n231\t            continue;\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n235\t\n236\t        // Handle scope transitions\n237\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n238\t        match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n239\t            Ordering::Greater => {\n240\t                // Entering deeper scope\n241\t                if let Some(KeyContext::ParentMapping { ref key }) = extract_key_context(line) {\n242\t                    // Add the parent key to current scope first\n243\t                    if let Err(e) = scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num_1index) {\n244\t                        return Err(e);\n245\t                    }\n246\t                    // Then enter the deeper scope\n247\t                    scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, Some(key.clone()));\n248\t                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Enter scope '{}'\", line_num_1index, key));\n249\t                    continue; // Parent keys already processed above\n250\t                } else {\n251\t                    // Not a parent mapping, just indent increase - enter anonymous scope\n252\t                    scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, None);\n253\t                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Enter scope at indent {}\", line_num_1index, indent));\n254\t                }\n255\t            }\n256\t            Ordering::Less => {\n257\t                // Exiting to parent scope\n258\t                scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n259\t                processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Exit to scope at indent {}\", line_num_1index, indent));\n260\t            }\n261\t            Ordering::Equal => {\n262\t                // Same scope - continue checking\n263\t            }\n264\t        }\n265\t\n266\t        // Extract and check key\n267\t        if let Some(key_context) = extract_key_context(line) {\n268\t            match key_context {\n269\t                KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } => {\n270\t Scope-Based Key Tracking Demonstration\n//!\n//! This example demonstrates the hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n//! system for YAML parsing, showing how it properly handles nested\n//! mappings and avoids false duplicate positives.\n\nuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started\n    start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    is_flow_style: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Calculate scope level from indentation\n        let scope_level = indent_level / self.base_indent;\n\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n        } else {\n            // Create new scope\n            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n\n        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Check if key exists before adding\n        if self.contains_key(key) {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n                key: key.to_string(),\n                scope_path,\n                first_line: scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line: line,\n            })\n        } else {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            scope.add_key(key);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct DuplicateKeyError {\n    key: String,\n    scope_path: String,\n    first_line: usize,\n    duplicate_line: usize,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n/// Key context classification\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\nenum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    InlineScalar { key: String, value: String },\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ParentMapping { key: String },\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ParentSequence { key: String },\n}\n\n/// Extract key context from a line\nfn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n\n    // Skip if key is empty\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n\n    Some(context)\n}\n\n/// Get leading whitespace length\nfn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n}\n\n/// Process YAML lines and detect duplicate keys\nfn process_yaml(yaml: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, DuplicateKeyError> {\n    let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n    let mut processed_keys = Vec::new();\n\n    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n        let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Skip empty lines and comments\n        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Handle scope transitions\n        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n        match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n            Ordering::Greater => {\n                // Entering deeper scope\n                if let Some(KeyContext::ParentMapping { ref key }) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Add the parent key to current scope first\n                    if let Err(e) = scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num_1index) {\n                        return Err(e);\n                    }\n                    // Then enter the deeper scope\n                    scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, Some(key.clone()));\n                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Enter scope '{}'\", line_num_1index, key));\n                    continue; // Parent keys already processed above\n                } else {\n                    // Not a parent mapping, just indent increase - enter anonymous scope\n                    scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num_1index, None);\n                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Enter scope at indent {}\", line_num_1index, indent));\n                }\n            }\n            Ordering::Less => {\n                // Exiting to parent scope\n                scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Exit to scope at indent {}\", line_num_1index, indent));\n            }\n            Ordering::Equal => {\n                // Same scope - continue checking\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Extract and check key\n        if let Some(key_context) = extract_key_context(line) {\n            match key_context {\n                KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } => {\n                    // Handled in scope transition above\n                }\n                KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n                    // Check for duplicate\n                    scope_stack.add_key(&key, line_num_1index)?;\n                    processed_keys.push(format!(\"Line {}: Added key '{}'\", line_num_1index, key));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(processed_keys)\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    println!(\"=== Scope-Based Key Tracking Demonstration ===\\n\");\n\n    // Example 1: Sibling mappings with same keys (should be OK)\n    println!(\"--- Example 1: Sibling Mappings ---\");\n    let yaml1 = \"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml1) {\n        Ok(actions) => {\n            println!(\"✓ No duplicate key errors (expected)\");\n            for action in actions {\n                println!(\"  {}\", action);\n            }\n        }\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✗ Unexpected error: {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n\n    // Example 2: Actual duplicate in same scope (should fail)\n    println!(\"--- Example 2: Actual Duplicate ---\");\n    let yaml2 = \"\nconfig:\n  host: localhost\n  host: duplicate\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml2) {\n        Ok(_) => println!(\"✗ Should have detected duplicate key\"),\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✓ Correctly detected duplicate:\\n  {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n\n    // Example 3: Deeply nested mappings\n    println!(\"--- Example 3: Deep Nesting ---\");\n    let yaml3 = \"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    level3:\n      key: value1\n    key: value2\n  key: value3\nkey: value4\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml3) {\n        Ok(actions) => {\n            println!(\"✓ No duplicate key errors (expected)\");\n            for action in actions {\n                println!(\"  {}\", action);\n            }\n        }\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✗ Unexpected error: {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n\n    // Example 4: Mixed scalar and mapping values\n    println!(\"--- Example 4: Mixed Values ---\");\n    let yaml4 = \"\nscalar_key: simple_value\nmapping_key:\n  nested: value\nanother_scalar: value2\n\";\n\n    match process_yaml(yaml4) {\n        Ok(actions) => {\n            println!(\"✓ No duplicate key errors (expected)\");\n            for action in actions {\n                println!(\"  {}\", action);\n            }\n        }\n        Err(e) => println!(\"✗ Unexpected error: {}\", e),\n    }\n\n    println!();\n    println!(\"=== Demonstration Complete ===\");\n}\n","numLines":372,"startLine":1,"totalLines":372}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8ee295a308414f6e8fd36c72","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n69\t///\n70\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n71\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n72\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n73\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n74\tpub struct Scope {\n75\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n76\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n77\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n78\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n79\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n80\t    pub start_line: usize,\n81\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n82\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n83\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n84\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n85\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n86\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n87\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n88\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n89\t}\n90\t\n91\timpl Scope {\n92\t    /// Create a new scope\n93\t    ///\n94\t    /// # Arguments\n95\t    ///\n96\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n97\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n98\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n99\t    ///\n100\t    /// # Examples\n101\t    ///\n102\t    /// ```\n103\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n104\t    ///\n105\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n106\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n107\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n108\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n109\t    /// ```\n110\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n111\t        Self {\n112\t            indent_level,\n113\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n114\t            start_line,\n115\t            parent_key,\n116\t            is_flow_style: false,\n117\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n118\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n119\t        }\n120\t    }\n121\t\n122\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n123\t    ///\n124\t    /// # Arguments\n125\t    ///\n126\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// # Returns\n129\t    ///\n130\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n131\t    ///\n132\t    /// # Examples\n133\t    ///\n134\t    /// ```\n135\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n140\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n141\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n142\t    /// ```\n143\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n144\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n145\t    }\n146\t\n147\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n148\t    ///\n149\t    /// # Arguments\n150\t    ///\n151\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n152\t    ///\n153\t    /// # Returns\n154\t    ///\n155\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n156\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n157\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n158\t    }\n159\t\n160\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n161\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n162\t        self.keys.len()\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n166\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n167\t        self.keys.clear();\n168\t    }\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n172\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n173\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n174\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n175\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n176\t        }\n177\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n178\t    }\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n182\t///\n183\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n184\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n185\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n186\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n187\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n188\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n189\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n190\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n191\t    base_indent: usize,\n192\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n193\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\timpl ScopeStack {\n197\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n198\t    ///\n199\t    /// # Arguments\n200\t    ///\n201\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n202\t    ///\n203\t    /// # Examples\n204\t    ///\n205\t    /// ```\n206\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n207\t    ///\n208\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n209\t    /// ```\n210\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n211\t        Self {\n212\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n213\t            base_indent,\n214\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n215\t        }\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n219\t    ///\n220\t    /// # Panics\n221\t    ///\n222\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n223\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n224\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n228\t    ///\n229\t    /// # Panics\n230\t    ///\n231\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n232\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n233\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n234\t    }\n235\t\n236\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n237\t    ///\n238\t    /// # Arguments\n239\t    ///\n240\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// # Returns\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n245\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n246\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n252\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n253\t    ///\n254\t    /// # Arguments\n255\t    ///\n256\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n257\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n258\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n259\t    ///\n260\t    /// # Examples\n261\t    ///\n262\t    /// ```\n263\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n264\t    ///\n265\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n266\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n267\t    /// ```\n268\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n269\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n270\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n271\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n272\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n273\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n274\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n275\t\n276\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n277\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n278\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n279\t        } else {\n280\t            // Create new scope\n281\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n282\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n283\t        }\n284\t    }\n285\t\n286\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n287\t    ///\n288\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n289\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n290\t    ///\n291\t    /// # Arguments\n292\t    ///\n293\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n294\t    ///\n295\t    /// # Examples\n296\t    ///\n297\t    /// ```\n298\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n299\t    ///\n300\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n301\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n302\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n303\t    /// ```\n304\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n305\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n306\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n307\t\n308\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n309\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n310\t            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n311\t            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n312\t            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n313\t        }\n314\t    }\n315\t\n316\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n317\t    ///\n318\t    /// # Arguments\n319\t    ///\n320\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n321\t    ///\n322\t    /// # Returns\n323\t    ///\n324\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n325\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n326\t        self.scopes.last()\n327\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n328\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n329\t    }\n330\t\n331\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n332\t    ///\n333\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n334\t    ///\n335\t    /// # Arguments\n336\t    ///\n337\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n338\t    ///\n339\t    /// # Returns\n340\t    ///\n341\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n342\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n343\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n344\t    }\n345\t\n346\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n347\t    ///\n348\t    /// # Arguments\n349\t    ///\n350\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n351\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n352\t    ///\n353\t    /// # Returns\n354\t    ///\n355\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n356\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n357\t    ///\n358\t    /// # Examples\n359\t    ///\n360\t    /// ```\n361\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n362\t    ///\n363\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n364\t    ///\n365\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n366\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n367\t    ///\n368\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n369\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n370\t    /// ```\n371\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n372\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n373\t\n374\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n375\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n376\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n377\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n378\t                key: key.to_string(),\n379\t                scope_path,\n380\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n381\t                duplicate_line: line,\n382\t            })\n383\t        } else {\n384\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n385\t            scope.add_key(key);\n386\t            Ok(())\n387\t        }\n388\t    }\n389\t\n390\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n391\t    ///\n392\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n393\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n394\t    ///\n395\t    /// # Returns\n396\t    ///\n397\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n398\t    ///\n399\t    /// # Examples\n400\t    ///\n401\t    /// ```\n402\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n403\t    ///\n404\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n405\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n406\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n407\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n408\t    /// ```\n409\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n410\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n411\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n412\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n413\t                path.push(key.clone());\n414\t            }\n415\t        }\n416\t        path.join(\".\")\n417\t    }\n418\t\n419\t    /// Get current indent level\n420\t    ///\n421\t    /// # Returns\n422\t    ///\n423\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n424\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n425\t        self.scopes.last()\n426\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n427\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n428\t    }\n429\t\n430\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n431\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n432\t        self.scopes.len()\n433\t    }\n434\t\n435\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n436\t    ///\n437\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n438\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n439\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n443\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n444\t        self.base_indent\n445\t    }\n446\t\n447\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n448\t    ///\n449\t    /// # Arguments\n450\t    ///\n451\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n452\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n453\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n454\t    }\n455\t\n456\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n457\t    ///\n458\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n459\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n460\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n461\t    ///\n462\t    /// # Arguments\n463\t    ///\n464\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n465\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Examples\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// ```\n470\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n471\t    ///\n472\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n473\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n474\t    /// ```\n475\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n476\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n477\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n478\t\n479\t        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n480\t        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n481\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n482\t            .unwrap_or(true);\n483\t\n484\t        if needs_new_scope {\n485\t            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n486\t            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n487\t            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n488\t            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n489\t            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n490\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n491\t        } else {\n492\t            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n493\t            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n494\t                scope.keys.clear();\n495\t                scope.start_line = line;\n496\t                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n497\t                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n498\t            }\n499\t        }\n500\t    }\n501\t\n502\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n503\t    ///\n504\t    /// # Returns\n505\t    ///\n506\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n507\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n508\t        self.scopes.last()\n509\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n510\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n511\t    }\n512\t}\n513\t\n514\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n515\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n516\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n517\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n518\t    }\n519\t}\n520\t\n521\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n522\t///\n523\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n524\t/// in the current scope.\n525\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n526\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n527\t    /// The duplicate key\n528\t    pub key: String,\n529\t    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n530\t    pub scope_path: String,\n531\t    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n532\t    pub first_line: usize,\n533\t    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n534\t    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\timpl DuplicateKeyError {\n538\t    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n539\t    ///\n540\t    /// # Arguments\n541\t    ///\n542\t    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n543\t    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n544\t    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n545\t    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n546\t    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n547\t        Self {\n548\t            key,\n549\t            scope_path,\n550\t            first_line,\n551\t            duplicate_line,\n552\t        }\n553\t    }\n554\t\n555\t    /// Get a formatted error message\n556\t    ///\n557\t    /// # Returns\n558\t    ///\n559\t    /// A human-readable error message\n560\t    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n561\t        format!(\n562\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n563\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n564\t        )\n565\t    }\n566\t}\n567\t\n568\timpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n569\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n570\t        write!(\n571\t            f,\n572\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n573\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n574\t        )\n575\t    }\n576\t}\n577\t\n578\timpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n579\t\n580\t/// Key context classification\n581\t///\n582\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n583\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n584\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n585\tpub enum KeyContext {\n586\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n587\t    ///\n588\t    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n589\t    InlineScalar {\n590\t        /// The key name\n591\t        key: String,\n592\t        /// The scalar value\n593\t        value: String,\n594\t    },\n595\t\n596\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n597\t    ///\n598\t    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n599\t    ParentMapping {\n600\t        /// The parent key name\n601\t        key: String,\n602\t    },\n603\t\n604\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n605\t    ///\n606\t    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n607\t    ParentSequence {\n608\t        /// The parent key name\n609\t        key: String,\n610\t    },\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\timpl KeyContext {\n614\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n615\t    ///\n616\t    /// # Returns\n617\t    ///\n618\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n619\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n620\t        match self {\n621\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n622\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n623\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n624\t        }\n625\t    }\n626\t\n627\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n628\t    ///\n629\t    /// # Returns\n630\t    ///\n631\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n632\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n633\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n637\t    ///\n638\t    /// # Returns\n639\t    ///\n640\t    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n641\t    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n642\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n643\t    }\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\timpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n647\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n648\t        match self {\n649\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n650\t                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n651\t            }\n652\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n653\t                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n654\t            }\n655\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n656\t                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n657\t            }\n658\t        }\n659\t    }\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t/// Extract key context from a line\n663\t///\n664\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n665\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n666\t///\n667\t/// # Arguments\n668\t///\n669\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n670\t///\n671\t/// # Returns\n672\t///\n673\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n674\t///\n675\t/// # Examples\n676\t///\n677\t/// ```\n678\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n679\t///\n680\t/// // Inline scalar\n681\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n682\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n683\t///\n684\t/// // Parent mapping\n685\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n686\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n687\t/// ```\n688\tpub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n689\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n690\t\n691\t    // Find colon position\n692\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n693\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n694\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n695\t\n696\t    // Skip if key is empty\n697\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n698\t    if key.is_empty() {\n699\t        return None;\n700\t    }\n701\t\n702\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n703\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n704\t        return None;\n705\t    }\n706\t\n707\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n708\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n709\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n710\t    } else {\n711\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n712\t            key: key.to_string(),\n713\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n714\t        }\n715\t    };\n716\t\n717\t    Some(context)\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n721\t///\n722\t/// # Arguments\n723\t///\n724\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n725\t///\n726\t/// # Returns\n727\t///\n728\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n729\t///\n730\t/// # Examples\n731\t///\n732\t/// ```\n733\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n734\t///\n735\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n736\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n737\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n738\t/// ```\n739\tpub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n740\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n741\t}\n742\t\n743\t#[cfg(test)]\n744\tmod tests {\n745\t    use super::*;\n746\t\n747\t    #[test]\n748\t    fn test_scope_creation() {\n749\t        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n750\t        assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n751\t        assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n752\t        assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n753\t        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n754\t        assert!(!scope.is_flow_style);\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    #[test]\n758\t    fn test_scope_add_key() {\n759\t        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n760\t\n761\t        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false);\n762\t        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false);\n763\t        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2);\n764\t\n765\t        // Duplicate detection\n766\t        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);\n767\t        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2); // Not added\n768\t    }\n769\t\n770\t    #[test]\n771\t    fn test_scope_contains_key() {\n772\t        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n773\t\n774\t        assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n775\t        scope.add_key(\"test\");\n776\t        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n777\t    }\n778\t\n779\t    #[test]\n780\t    fn test_scope_stack_creation() {\n781\t        let stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n782\t        assert_eq!(stack.base_indent(), 2);\n783\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1); // Root scope\n784\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n785\t    }\n786\t\n787\t    #[test]\n788\t    fn test_scope_stack_enter_exit() {\n789\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n790\t\n791\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n792\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n793\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n794\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n795\t\n796\t        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n797\t        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n798\t        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n804\t    }\n805\t\n806\t    #[test]\n807\t    fn test_scope_stack_add_key() {\n808\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n809\t\n810\t        // scope\n815\t        assert!(stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 2).is_err());\n816\t\n817\t        // Different key succeeds\n818\t        assert!(stack.add_key(\"another_key\", 3).is_ok());\n819\t    }\n820\t\n821\t    #[test]\n822\t    fn test_scope_stack_add_key_in_nested_scope() {\n823\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n824\t\n825\t        // Add to root\n826\t        stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n827\t\n828\t        // Enter nested scope\n829\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n830\t\n831\t        // Same key name is OK in assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key\"));\n849\t    }\n850\t\n851\t    #[test]\n852\t    fn test_duplicate_key_error() {\n853\t        let error = DuplicateKeyError::new(\n854\t            \"host\".to_string(),\n855\t            \"services.web\".to_string(),\n856\t            5,\n857\t            10,\n858\t        );\n859\t\n860\t        assert_eq!(error.key, \"host\");\n861\t        assert_eq!(error.scope_path, \"services.web\");\n862\t        assert_eq!(error.first_line, 5);\n863\t        assert_eq!(error.duplicate_line, 10);\n864\t\n865\t        let msg = error.message();\n866\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"duplicate key\"));\n867\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"host\"));\n868\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"services.web\"));\n869\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"Line 10\"));\n870\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"Line 5\"));\n871\t    }\n872\t\n873\t    #[test]\n874\t    fn test_key_context_inline_scalar() {\n875\t        let ctx = assert!(ctx.is_inline_scalar());\n879\t        assert!(!ctx.is_parent_key());\n880\t    }\n881\t\n882\t    #[test]\n883\t    fn test_key_context_parent_mapping() {\n884\t        let ctx = assert!(!ctx.is_inline_scalar());\n888\t        assert!(ctx.is_parent_key());\n889\t    }\n890\t\n891\t    #[test]\n892\t    fn test_extract_key_context_invalid() {\n893\t        // No colon\n894\t        assert!(extract_key_context(\"no key here\").is_none());\n895\t\n896\t        // Empty key\n897\t        assert!(extract_key_context(\": value\").is_none());\n898\t\n899\t        // Flow style (contains braces)\n900\t        assert!(extract_key_context(\"{key: value}\").is_none());\n901\t    }\n902\t\n903\t    #[test]\n904\t    fn test_get_leading_whitespace_length() {\n905\t        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n906\t        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n907\t        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    key: value\"), 4);\n908\t        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"\\tkey: value\"), 1); // Tab counts as 1\n909\t    }\n910\t\n911\t    #[test]\n912\t    fn test_scope_stack_sibling_mappings() {\n913\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n914\t\n915\t        // First sibling\n916\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n917\t        stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n918\t        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n919\t\n920\t        // Second sibling at same level\n921\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n922\t        // Same key should be OK (different scope)\n923\t        assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).is_ok());\n924\t    }\n925\t\n926\t    #[test]\n927\t    fn test_scope_stack_display() {\n928\t        let stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n929\t        let display = format!(\"{}\", stack);\n930\t        assert!(display.contains(\"ScopeStack\"));\n931\t        assert!(display.contains(\"depth=\"));\n932\t        assert!(display.contains(\"base_indent=\"));\n933\t    }\n934\t\n935\t    #[test]\n936\t    fn test_scope_display() {\n937\t        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n938\t        let display = format!(\"{}\", scope);\n939\t        assert!(display.contains(\"Scope\"));\n940\t        assert!(display.contains(\"indent=2\"));\n941\t        assert!(display.contains(\"parent=parent\"));\n942\t    }\n943\t\n944\t    #[test]\n945\t    fn test_enter_sequence_scope() {\n946\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n947\t\n948\t        // Enter first sequence item\n949\t        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n950\t        assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n951\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n952\t\n953\t        // Add a key to the first sequence item\n954\t        stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n955\t\n956\t        // Enter second sequence item\n961\t        stack.add_key(\"name\", 4).unwrap();\n962\t\n963\t        // But duplicate in same sequence item should fail\n964\t        assert!(stack.add_key(\"name\", 5).is_err());\n965\t    }\n966\t\n967\t    #[test]\n968\t    fn test_sequence_scope_with_unique_ids() {\n969\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n970\t\n971\t        // Enter first sequence item\n972\t        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n973\t        let first_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n974\t        assert_eq!(first_id, Some(1));\n975\t\n976\t        // Enter second sequence ScopeStack::new(2);\n988\t\n989\t        // Enter first sequence item and add keys\n990\t        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n991\t        stack.add_key(\"first\", 2).unwrap();\n992\t
