  stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n997\t        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n998\t\n999\t        // Keys from first item should not be in second\n1000\t        stack.add_key(\"first\", 6).unwrap();\n1001\t        stack.add_key(\"second\", 7).unwrap();\n1002\t    }\n1003\t\n1004\t    #[test]\n1005\t    fn test_mixed_regular_and_sequence_scopes() {\n1006\t        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n1007\t\n1008\t        // Enter regular scope\n1009\t        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n1010\t        stack.add_key(\"item1\", 2).unwrap();\n1011\t\n1012\t        // Enter sequence scope within regular scope\n1013\t        stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 3);\n1014\t        stack.add_key(\"name\", 4).unwrap();\n1015\t\n1016\t        // Exit sequence scope back to regular\n1017\t        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n1018\t        assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n1019\t\n1020\t        // Add another key to regular scope\n1021\t        stack.add_key(\"item2\", 5).unwrap();\n1022\t    }\n1023\t}\n1024\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a81f39b3-ab0b-4b57-9c32-d16f2f699c00","uuid":"63a26638-9276-4119-939f-7e1aa0e01ddb","timestamp":"2026-07-13T18:44:36.474Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs","content":"//! Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!\n//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n//!\n//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n//!\n//! // Add keys to current scope\n//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n//!\n//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n//! ```\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n///\n/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n            in_sequence_context: false,\n            sequence_item_id: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.keys.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n        self.keys.clear();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n        }\n        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n///\n/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n        } else {\n            // Create new scope\n            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n    /// ```\n    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n\n        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n    ///\n    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    ///\n    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n    ///\n    /// // Second occurrence fails\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Check if key exists before adding\n        if self.contains_key(key) {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n                key: key.to_string(),\n                scope_path,\n                first_line: scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line: line,\n            })\n        } else {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            scope.add_key(key);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    ///\n    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A string representing the scope path\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n    ///\n    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n    }\n\n    /// Get the base indentation size\n    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.base_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Set the base indentation size\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n\n        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(true);\n\n        if needs_new_scope {\n            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n        } else {\n            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n                scope.keys.clear();\n                scope.start_line = line;\n                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n///\n/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n/// in the current scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// The duplicate key\n    pub key: String,\n    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    pub scope_path: String,\n    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n    pub first_line: usize,\n    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n}\n\nimpl DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            key,\n            scope_path,\n            first_line,\n            duplicate_line,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a formatted error message\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A human-readable error message\n    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n\n/// Key context classification\n///\n/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n    InlineScalar {\n        /// The key name\n        key: String,\n        /// The scalar value\n        value: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n    ParentMapping {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n    ParentSequence {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl KeyContext {\n    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The key name as a string slice\n    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract key context from a line\n///\n/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n///\n/// // Inline scalar\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n///\n/// // Parent mapping\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n/// ```\npub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n\n    // Skip if key is empty\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n\n    Some(context)\n}\n\n/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n///\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n/// ```\npub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_creation() {\n        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n        assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n        assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n        assert!(!scope.is_flow_style);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_add_key() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false);\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false);\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2);\n\n        // Duplicate detection\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2); // Not added\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_contains_key() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n        scope.add_key(\"test\");\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_creation() {\n        let stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.base_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1); // Root scope\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_enter_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent.child\");\n\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_add_key() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Add to root scope\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1).is_ok());\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"root_key\"));\n\n        // Duplicate detection in same scope\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 2).is_err());\n\n        // Different key succeeds\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"another_key\", 3).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_add_key_in_nested_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Add to root\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n\n        // Enter nested scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n        // Same key name is OK in different scope\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).is_ok());\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_reset() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n\n        stack.reset();\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1);\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_error() {\n        let error = DuplicateKeyError::new(\n            \"host\".to_string(),\n            \"services.web\".to_string(),\n            5,\n            10,\n        );\n\n        assert_eq!(error.key, \"host\");\n        assert_eq!(error.scope_path, \"services.web\");\n        assert_eq!(error.first_line, 5);\n        assert_eq!(error.duplicate_line, 10);\n\n        let msg = error.message();\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"duplicate key\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"host\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"services.web\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"Line 10\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"Line 5\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_context_inline_scalar() {\n        let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n        assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"host\");\n        assert!(ctx.is_inline_scalar());\n        assert!(!ctx.is_parent_key());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_context_parent_mapping() {\n        let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n        assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"services\");\n        assert!(!ctx.is_inline_scalar());\n        assert!(ctx.is_parent_key());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_key_context_invalid() {\n        // No colon\n        assert!(extract_key_context(\"no key here\").is_none());\n\n        // Empty key\n        assert!(extract_key_context(\": value\").is_none());\n\n        // Flow style (contains braces)\n        assert!(extract_key_context(\"{key: value}\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_get_leading_whitespace_length() {\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    key: value\"), 4);\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"\\tkey: value\"), 1); // Tab counts as 1\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_sibling_mappings() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // First sibling\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n        // Second sibling at same level\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n        // Same key should be OK (different scope)\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_display() {\n        let stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let display = format!(\"{}\", stack);\n        assert!(display.contains(\"ScopeStack\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"depth=\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"base_indent=\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_display() {\n        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        let display = format!(\"{}\", scope);\n        assert!(display.contains(\"Scope\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"indent=2\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"parent=parent\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enter_sequence_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Enter first sequence item\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n\n        // Add a key to the first sequence item\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        // Enter second sequence item (same indent level)\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n        assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n        // Same key should be OK in different sequence item\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", 4).unwrap();\n\n        // But duplicate in same sequence item should fail\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"name\", 5).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_with_unique_ids() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Enter first sequence item\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        let first_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n        assert_eq!(first_id, Some(1));\n\n        // Enter second sequence item\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n        let second_id = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n        assert_eq!(second_id, Some(2));\n\n        // IDs should be different\n        assert_ne!(first_id, second_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_clears_keys() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Enter first sequence item and add keys\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n        stack.add_key(\"first\", 2).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"second\", 3).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n\n        // Enter second sequence item (should clear keys from first)\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n\n        // Keys from first item should not be in second\n        stack.add_key(\"first\", 6).unwrap();\n        stack.add_key(\"second\", 7).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_mixed_regular_and_sequence_scopes() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Enter regular scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"item1\", 2).unwrap();\n\n stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n        // Add another key to regular scope\n        stack.add_key(\"item2\", 5).unwrap();\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_46f4cc0857114ab0af6f76d4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Scope Representation Data Structures\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThe scope representation module (`src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs`) provides hierarchical scope tracking for YAML parsing, enabling accurate duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts.\n6\t\n7\t## Architecture\n8\t\n9\t### Core Components\n10\t\n11\t1. **Scope** - Represents a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n12\t2. **ScopeStack** - Hierarchical stack managing active scopes during parsing\n13\t3. **KeyContext** - Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n14\t4. **DuplicateKeyError** - Error type for duplicate key detection\n15\t\n16\t### Scope Structure\n17\t\n18\t```rust\n19\tpub struct Scope {\n20\t    pub indent_level: usize,        // Leading spaces\n21\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,      // Keys in this scope\n22\t    pub start_line: usize,           // Where scope started\n23\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>, // Parent key name\n24\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,         // Flow-style mapping\n25\t}\n26\t```\n27\t\n28\t### ScopeStack Operations\n29\t\n30\t- `enter_scope(indent, line, parent_key)` - Enter nested scope\n31\t- `exit_to_scope(target_indent)` - Exit to parent scope\n32\t- `add_key(key, line)` - Add key to current scope (detects duplicates)\n33\t- `get_scope_path()` - Get dot-separated path (e.g., \"services.web\")\n34\t\n35\t## Usage Example\n36\t\n37\t```rust\n38\tuse armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{ScopeStack, DuplicateKeyError};\n39\t\n40\tlet mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n41\t\n42\t// Enter scope when encountering parent mapping\n43\tstack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n44\t\n45\t// Add keys to current scope\n46\tstack.add_key(\"web\", 2)?;\n47\tstack.add_key(\"database\", 3)?;\n48\t\n49\t// Detect duplicate in same scope\n50\tlet result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n51\tassert!(result.is_err()); // DuplicateKeyError\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\t## Key Benefits\n55\t\n56\t1. **Accurate Duplicate Detection** - Only detects true duplicates within the same scope\n57\t2. **Nested Scope Support** - Handles deeply nested YAML structures correctly\n58\t3. **Scope Path Tracking** - Provides human-readable scope paths for error messages\n59\t4. **Flow Style Support** - Handles both block and flow-style YAML mappings\n60\t\n61\t## Integration Points\n62\t\n63\tThe scope module integrates with:\n64\t- `line_parser` - For extracting key context from YAML lines\n65\t- `syntax_validator` - Represents a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n2. **ScopeStack** - Hierarchical stack managing active scopes during parsing\n3. **KeyContext** - Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n4. **DuplicateKeyError** - Error type for duplicate key detection\n\n### Scope Structure\n\n```rust\npub struct Scope {\n    pub indent_level: usize,        // Leading spaces\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,      // Keys in this scope\n    pub start_line: usize,           // Where scope started\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>, // Parent key name\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,         // Flow-style mapping\n}\n```\n\n### ScopeStack Operations\n\n- `enter_scope(indent, line, parent_key)` - Enter nested scope\n- `exit_to_scope(target_indent)` - Exit to parent scope\n- `add_key(key, line)` - Add key to current scope (detects duplicates)\n- `get_scope_path()` - Get dot-separated path (e.g., \"services.web\")\n\n## Usage Example\n\n```rust\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{ScopeStack, DuplicateKeyError};\n\nlet mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n\n// Enter scope when encountering parent mapping\nstack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n// Add keys to current scope\nstack.add_key(\"web\", 2)?;\nstack.add_key(\"database\", 3)?;\n\n// Detect duplicate in same scope\nlet result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\nassert!(result.is_err()); // DuplicateKeyError\n```\n\n## Key Benefits\n\n1. **Accurate Duplicate Detection** - Only detects true duplicates within the same scope\n2. **Nested Scope Support** - Handles deeply nested YAML structures correctly\n3. **Scope Path Tracking** - Provides human-readable scope paths for error messages\n4. **Flow Style Support** - Handles both block and flow-style YAML mappings\n\n## Integration Points\n\nThe scope module integrates with:\n- `line_parser` - For extracting key context from YAML lines\n- `syntax_validator` - For validation during parsing\n- `parser` - For use during YAML parsing operations\n\n## Testing\n\nThe module includes 16 comprehensive tests covering:\n- Scope creation and key management\n- Stack enter/exit operations\n- Duplicate detection in same vs. different scopes\n- Sibling mapping handling\n- Key context extraction\n- Error handling and display\n","numLines":77,"startLine":1,"totalLines":77}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a3d65c76ef394e8a859b4ed6","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Scope-Based Key Tracking Design for ARMOR YAML Parser\n2\t\n3\t## Executive Summary\n4\t\n5\tThis document outlines the design for a scope-based key tracking system that properly handles YAML mapping keys within their correct scope context. The current implementation has limitations in handling nested structures and scope transitions. This design provides a hierarchical scope system with proper key isolation and comprehensive duplicate detection.\n6\t\n7\t## Current Implementation Analysis\n8\t\n9\t### Existing Structure (from `syntax_detector.rs`)\n10\t\n11\t```rust\n12\tstruct StructureState {\n13\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,  // Tracks structure types only\n14\t    current_keys: HashSet<String>,          // Single-level key tracking\n15\t    prev_indent: usize,                     // Previous indentation level\n16\t}\n17\t```\n18\t\n19\t### Current Limitations\n20\t\n21\t1. **No Scope Hierarchy**: Only tracks keys at one level at a time\n22\t2. **Aggressive Key Clearing**: Clears all keys when indentation decreases\n23\t3. **No Scope Preservation**: Loses parent scope keys when entering nested structures\n24\t4. **Context Type Only**: `context_stack` only tracks structure types, not keys\n25\t5. **False Positives**: May report duplicates when keys appear in different scopes\n26\t\n27\t### Current Behavior\n28\t\n29\t```yaml\n30\t# Current implementation behavior:\n31\tservices:\n32\t  web:\n33\t    host: localhost      # Key \"host\" added to current_keys\n34\t    port: 8080           # Key \"port\" added to current_keys\n35\t  database:\n36\t    host: db.example.com  # ERROR: \"host\" reported as duplicate!\n37\t    port: 5432            # ERROR: \"port\" reported as duplicate!\n38\t```\n39\t\n40\tThe current implementation incorrectly reports duplicates because it doesn't distinguish between different mapping scopes (`services.web` vs `services.database`).\n41\t\n42\t## Proposed Design\n43\t\n44\t### Core Concept: Hierarchical Scope Stack\n45\t\n46\tA **scope** represents a mapping context where keys are defined and must be unique. Each scope is associated with a specific indentation level and maintains its own set of keys.\n47\t\n48\t#### Scope Definition\n49\t\n50\t```rust\n51\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n52\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n53\tstruct Scope {\n54\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n55\t    indent_level: usize,\n56\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n57\t    keys: HashSet<String>,\n58\t    /// Line number where this scope started (for error reporting)\n59\t    start_line: usize,\n60\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n61\t    parent_key: Option<String>,\n62\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n63\t    is_flow_style: bool,\n64\t}\n65\t```\n66\t\n67\t#### Scope Stack\n68\t\n69\t```rust\n70\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n71\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n72\tstruct ScopeStack {\n73\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n74\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n75\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n76\t    base_indent: usize,\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\timpl ScopeStack {\n80\t    /// Get the scope for a specific indentation level\n81\t    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope>;\n82\t    \n83\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n84\t    fn current_scope(&self) -> Option<&Scope>;\n85\t    \n86\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n87\t    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>);\n88\t    \n89\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n90\t    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize);\n91\t    \n92\t    /// Check if a key already exists in current scope\n93\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool;\n94\t    \n95\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n96\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: String, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError>;\n97\t}\n98\t```\n99\t\n100\t### Scope Transition Algorithm\n101\t\n102\t#### 1. Entering a Scope (Indentation Increase)\n103\t\n104\t```yaml\n105\tservices:\n106\t  web:              # ← ENTER scope for \"services.web\"\n107\t    host: localhost\n108\t    port: 8080\n109\t```\n110\t\n111\t**Algorithm:**\n112\t```rust\n113\tfn on_indent_increase(indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n114\t    // Calculate scope level from indentation\n115\t    let scope_level = indent_level / base_indent;\n116\t    \n117\t    // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n118\t    if let Some(existing_scope) = get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n119\t        // Re-enter existing scope (e.g., sibling mapping)\n120\t        // Don't clear keys - they persist within the scope\n121\t        set_current_scope(existing_scope);\n122\t    } else {\n123\t        // Create new scope for this indentation level\n124\t        let new_scope = Scope {\n125\t            indent_level,\n126\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n127\t            start_line: line,\n128\t            parent_key,\n129\t            is_flow_style: false,\n130\t        };\n131\t        push_scope(new_scope);\n132\t    }\n133\t}\n134\t```\n135\t\n136\t#### 2. Exiting a Scope (Indentation Decrease)\n137\t\n138\t```yaml\n139\tservices:\n140\t  web:\n141\t    host: localhost\n142\t    port: 8080\n143\tdatabase:           # ← EXIT scope for \"services.web\"\n144\t  host: db.example.com\n145\t```\n146\t\n147\t**Algorithm:**\n148\t```rust\n149\tfn on_indent_decrease(new_indent_level: usize) {\n150\t    // Pop scopes until we find the target scope\n151\t    while current_scope().indent_level > new_indent_level {\n152\t        pop_scope();\n153\t    }\n154\t    \n155\t    // Set current to the scope matching new_indent_level\n156\t    set_current_scope(get_scope_at_level(new_indent_level));\n157\t}\n158\t```\n159\t\n160\t**Key Preservation**: When exiting a scope, we **preserve** the scope and its keys. If we re-enter that scope later (e.g., a sibling mapping), the keys are still available for duplicate checking.\n161\t\n162\t### Key Detection and Classification\n163\t\n164\t#### Key Context Types\n165\t\n166\t```rust\n167\t/// Classification of how a key is used in the YAML document\n168\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n169\tenum KeyContext {\n170\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n171\t    InlineScalar {\n172\t        key: String,\n173\t        value: String,\n174\t    },\n175\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n176\t    ParentMapping {\n177\t        key: String,\n178\t    },\n179\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n180\t    ParentSequence {\n181\t        key: String,\n182\t    },\n183\t    /// Key in flow mapping: \"{key: value, key2: value2}\"\n184\t    FlowMapping {\n185\t        key: String,\n186\t    },\n187\t    /// Key with flow sequence value: \"key: [val1, val2]\"\n188\t    FlowSequenceValue {\n189\t        key: String,\n190\t    },\n191\t    /// Key in explicit mapping: \"? key\\n: value\"\n192\t    ExplicitMapping {\n193\t        key: String,\n194\t    },\n195\t}\n196\t```\n197\t\n198\t#### Key Extraction Algorithm\n199\t\n200\t```rust\n201\tfn extract_key_context(line: &str, line_num: usize) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n202\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n203\t    \n204\t    // Skip if not a key-value line\n205\t    if !trimmed.contains(':') {\n206\t        return None;\n207\t    }\n208\t    \n209\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':').unwrap();\n210\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n211\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos+1..];\n212\t    \n213\t    // Skip if key is empty or contains special characters\n214\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n215\t    if key.is_empty() || key.contains(['?', '&', '*', '!', '[', ']', '{', '}']) {\n216\t        return None;\n217\t    }\n218\t    \n219\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n220\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n221\t        // \"key:\\n  ...\" → Parent key with nested content\n222\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n223\t    } else if after_colon.contains('[') {\n224\t        // \"key: [val1, val2]\" → Key with flow sequence\n225\t        KeyContext::FlowSequenceValue { key: key.to_string() }\n226\t    } else if after_colon.contains('{') {\n227\t        // \"key: {nested: value}\" → Key with flow mapping\n228\t        KeyContext::FlowMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n229\t    } else {\n230\t        // \"key: value\" → Inline scalar value\n231\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n232\t            key: key.to_string(),\n233\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n234\t        }\n235\t    };\n236\t    \n237\t    Some(context)\n238\t}\n239\t```\n240\t\n241\t### Duplicate Detection Algorithm\n242\t\n243\t```rust\n244\tfn detect_duplicate_keys(\n245\t    scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack,\n246\t    line: &str,\n247\t    line_num: usize,\n248\t) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n249\t    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n250\t    \n251\t    // Skip flow-style contexts (handled separately)\n252\t    if is_in_flow_context(line) {\n253\t        return errors;\n254\t    }\n255\t    \n256\t    let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n257\t    \n258\t    // Handle scope transitions\n259\t    match indent.cmp(&current_indent_level()) {\n260\t        Ordering::Greater => {\n261\t            // Entering deeper scope\n262\t            if let Some(KeyContext::ParentMapping { key }) = extract_key_context(line, line_num) {\n263\t                scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, Some(key));\n264\t            }\n265\t            return errors; // Parent keys don't create duplicate checks\n266\t        }\n267\t        Ordering::Less => {\n268\t            // Exiting to parent scope\n269\t            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n270\t        }\n271\t        Ordering::Equal => {\n272\t            // Same scope - continue checking for duplicates\n273\t        }\n274\t    }\n275\t    \n276\t    // Extract and check key\n277\t    if let Some(key_context) = extract_key_context(line, line_num) {\n278\t        match key_context {\n279\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } => {\n280\t                // Parent keys create new scope, handled above\n281\t            }\n282\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } |\n283\t            KeyContext::FlowMapping { key, .. } |\n284\t            KeyContext::FlowSequenceValue { key, .. } => {\n285\t                // Check for duplicate in current scope\n286\t                if scope_stack.contains_key(&key) {\n287\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n288\t                        format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n289\t                        format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope\", key)\n290\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_code(ErrorCode::KEY_DUPLICATE));\n291\t                } else {\n292\t                    scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num);\n293\t                }\n294\t            }\n295\t            _ => {}\n296\t        }\n297\t    }\n298\t    \n299\t    errors\n300\t}\n301\t```\n302\t\n303\t## Handling Complex Scenarios\n304\t\n305\t### Scenario 1: Deeply Nested Mappings\n306\t\n307\t```yaml\n308\tlevel1:\n309\t  level2:\n310\t    level3:\n311\t      key1: value1\n312\t      key2: value2\n313\t    key3: value3\n314\t  key4: value4\n315\tkey5: value5\n316\t```\n317\t\n318\t**Scope Stack Evolution:**\n319\t\n320\t1. Line 1 (`level1:`): Enter scope level 0 with parent_key=\"level1\"\n321\t2. Line 2 (`  level2:`): Enter scope level 2 with parent_key=\"level2\"\n322\t3. Line 3 (`    level3:`): Enter scope level 4 with parent_key=\"level3\"\n323\t4. Line 4 (`      key1:`): Add \"key1\" to scope at level 4\n324\t5. Line 5 (`      key2:`): Add \"key2\" to scope at level 4\n325\t6. Line 6 (`    key3:`): Exit to scope level 2, add \"key3\"\n326\t7. Line 7 (`  key4:`): Exit to scope level 0, add \"key4\"\n327\t8. Line 8 (`key5:`): Exit to root scope, add \"key5\"\n328\t\n329\t**Result**: Each \"key\" is tracked in its appropriate scope, no false duplicates.\n330\t\n331\t### Scenario 2: Sibling Mappings with Same Keys\n332\t\n333\t```yaml\n334\tservices:\n335\t  web:\n336\t    host: localhost\n337\t    port: 8080\n338\t  database:\n339\t    host: db.example.com\n340\t    port: 5432\n341\t```\n342\t\n343\t**Scope Stack Evolution:**\n344\t\n345\t1. Line 1 (`services:`): Root scope (level 0)\n346\t2. Line 2 (`  web:`): Enter scope level 2, parent_key=\"web\"\n347\t3. Line 3 (`    host:`): Add \"host\" to scope level 4\n348\t4. Line 4 (`    port:`): Add \"port\" to scope level 4\n349\t5. Line 5 (`  database:`): Exit to scope level 2, **preserve scope level 4 keys**\n350\t6. Line 6 (`    host:`): **Enter existing scope level 4**, check \"host\" against **fresh keys**\n351\t7. Line 7 (`    port:`): Add \"port\" to scope level 4\n352\t\n353\t**Critical Decision**: When exiting scope level 4 (going to level 2), do we:\n354\t- **Option A**: Clear the scope level 4 keys (current behavior)\n355\t- **Option B**: Preserve the scope level 4 keys for potential re-entry\n356\t\n357\t**Correct Behavior is Option A**: Each sibling mapping is a **new scope**. The \"host\" in `services.web` and the \"host\" in `services.database` are in **different scopes**, so they should both be allowed.\n358\t\n359\t**Revised Algorithm**:\n360\t```rust\n361\tfn on_indent_decrease(new_indent_level: usize) {\n362\t    // Clear all scopes deeper than new_indent_level\n363\t    scopes.retain(|scope| scope.indent_level <= new_indent_level);\n364\t    set_current_scope(new_indent_level);\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\tfn on_indent_increase(indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n368\t    // Always create a FRESH scope when entering a deeper level\n369\t    let new_scope = Scope {\n370\t        indent_level,\n371\t        keys: HashSet::new(),  // Fresh keys\n372\t        start_line: line,\n373\t        parent_key,\n374\t        is_flow_style: false,\n375\t    };\n376\t    push_scope(new_scope);\n377\t}\n378\t```\n379\t\n380\t### Scenario 3: Mixed Scalar and Mapping Values\n381\t\n382\t```yaml\n383\tsimple_key: scalar_value\n384\tmapping_key:\n385\t  nested1: value1\n386\t  nested2: value2\n387\tsequence_key:\n388\t  - item1\n389\t  - item2\n390\tflow_key: {inline: value, another: value2}\n391\t```\n392\t\n393\t**Handling**:\n394\t- `simple_key`: Add \"simple_key\" to root scope\n395\t- `mapping_key`: Add \"mapping_key\" to root scope, **then** enter nested scope\n396\t- `sequence_key`: Add \"sequence_key\" to root scope, enter sequence scope\n397\t- `flow_key`: Add \"flow_key\" to root scope, parse flow mapping separately\n398\t\n399\t### Scenario 4: Flow-Style Mappings\n400\t\n401\t```yaml\n402\tinline: {key1: value1, key2: value2}\n403\tmultiline: {\n404\t  key3: value3,\n405\t  key4: value4\n406\t}\n407\t```\n408\t\n409\t**Handling**:\n410\t- Detect flow context by checking for `{` character\n411\t- Extract keys using specialized flow parser\n412\t- Track duplicates within the flow mapping scope only\n413\t- Don't mix flow-style keys with block-style keys\n414\t\n415\t## Migration Path from Current Implementation\n416\t\n417\t### Phase 1: Add Scope Stack Alongside Current Implementation\n418\t\n419\t**Changes to `StructureState`:**\n420\t\n421\t```rust\n422\tstruct StructureState {\n423\t    // NEW: Hierarchical scope tracking\n424\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n425\t    \n426\t    // LEGACY: Keep for backward compatibility during migration\n427\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n428\t    current_keys: HashSet<String>,\n429\t    prev_indent: usize,\n430\t    \n431\t    // Migration flag\n432\t    use_legacy_tracking: bool,\n433\t}\n434\t```\n435\t\n436\t**Dual Implementation:**\n437\t```rust\n438\tfn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n439\t    if self.use_legacy_tracking {\n440\t        // Use current implementation\n441\t        self.legacy_detect_duplicates(line, line_num, errors);\n442\t    } else {\n443\t        // Use new scope-based implementation\n444\t        self.scope_based_detect_duplicates(line, line_num, errors);\n445\t    }\n446\t}\n447\t```\n448\t\n449\t### Phase 2: Feature Flag for New Implementation\n450\t\n451\t**Configuration Update:**\n452\t\n453\t```rust\n454\tpub struct DetectorConfig {\n455\t    // ... existing config ...\n456\t    \n457\t    /// Enable scope-based key tracking (Phase 2 feature)\n458\t    pub use_scope_based_key_tracking: bool,\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n462\t    fn default() -> Self {\n463\t        Self {\n464\t            // ... existing defaults ...\n465\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: false,  // Disabled by default during migration\n466\t        }\n467\t    }\n468\t}\n469\t```\n470\t\n471\t### Phase 3:      let yaml = \"\n481\tservices:\n482\t  web:\n483\t    host: localhost\n484\t  database:\n485\t    host: db.example.com\n486\t\";\n487\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n488\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n489\t            ..Default::default()\n490\t        });\n491\t        let errors = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n503\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n504\t            ..Default::default()\n505\t        });\n506\t        let errors = SyntaxDetector::with_config(DetectorConfig {\n521\t            use_scope_based_key_tracking: true,\n522\t            ..Default::default()\n523\t        });\n524\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n525\t        assert!(errors.is_empty(), \"Should handle deep nesting correctly\");\n526\t    }\n527\t}\n528\t```\n529\t\n530\t### Phase 4: Gradual Rollout\n531\t\n532\t1. **Week 1-2**: Feature flag = false (current behavior)\n533\t2. **Week 3-4**: Feature flag = true in test environments only\n534\t3. **Week 5-6**: Monitor error reports, tune
